name: dtln
architecture: dual-stage LSTM (breizhn/DTLN) — stage 1 predicts a magnitude mask, stage 2 refines the result in the time domain; separate LSTM state per stage
framing: rectangular (no analysis/synthesis window) — model trained end-to-end against this exact framing
source: https://github.com/breizhn/DTLN — standard public ONNX export (tensor names input_2/input_3/activation_2/tf_op_layer_stack_2 for stage 1, input_4/input_5/conv1d_3/tf_op_layer_stack_5 for stage 2; no embedded metadata, no local provenance file, but these are exactly the tensor names of breizhn's published export)
notes: Different architecture family from GTCRN/DPDFNet (dual-LSTM vs. STFT-domain complex mask), added as a second independent option to compare vocal retention against. Offline bench shows the same shared-limitation pattern as the other enhancers here (strong on noise, weak on music/speech) rather than a clear win — by-ear comparison still pending.
